message:
perf(label): route `br label list` and `list-all` through read-only fast-open

`br label list` (without an issue id) and `br label list-all` only
read the unique-labels view of the database — no writes, no
issue-id resolution, no auto-import side-effects. Previously they
went through the full `commands::label::execute` path, which opens
storage in the standard read-write mode and runs the auto-import /
auto-flush hooks.

Reroutes the two listing variants through the existing read-only
fast-open path so:
  - The command no longer waits on the auto-import probe (saves
    ~tens of ms for a no-op probe in projects with no JSONL drift).
  - Concurrent agents are no longer serialized behind a write
    transaction that the read-only command never needed.
  - The DB connection is opened with the existing read-only
    optimizations (skip the WAL-checkpoint check, skip the
    flush-pending logic).

Changes:

- src/cli/commands/label.rs:
  - New `execute_with_storage(command, json, ctx, storage) -> Result<bool>`
    public entry point. Returns Ok(true) when the subcommand is one
    of the read-only listings (`ListAll`, or `List` without an
    `issue` argument) and was handled. Returns Ok(false) for
    `Add`/`Remove`/`Rename` and for `List(args)` when an issue id
    is given — those paths still need the full router with config
    layer / overrides, so the caller falls back to the original
    `execute` function.
  - Extracts the previously-inlined "list all unique labels" branch
    of `label_list` into a new `label_list_unique(storage, ctx)`
    helper. Both `label_list` and the new `execute_with_storage`
    fast path call it, so JSON / TOON / quiet / Rich / plain text
    rendering stays in one place. Behavior is identical to the
    inlined form (same query, same modes, same empty-state
    message).

- src/main.rs:
  - The `Commands::Label { command }` arm in the dispatcher now
    consults the already-opened `storage_result` — when storage is
    available AND `execute_with_storage(...)` returns
    `Ok(true)`, the command is done. Otherwise it falls back to the
    original `commands::label::execute(...)` invocation. The
    fallback covers commands not yet wired through the fast path
    AND the case where read-only fast-open was disabled
    (`--no-fast-open` / unsupported-config combinations) and
    `storage_result` is `None`.
  - `supports_read_only_fast_open` matches add the `Label { command:
    List(_) | ListAll }` variants alongside `List` / `Ready` /
    `Stale` / `Stats` / `Status` / `Comments`.
  - `supports_auto_import_read_only_probe` matches add the same
    variants — the probe is what enables the fast-open code path
    to skip the JSONL-drift import and proceed directly to the
    cached SQLite view.
  - Two new test cases:
    - `label list` and `label list-all` produce overrides with
      `read_only_fast_open == true`.
    - `label add bd-abc --label ops` (a write command) keeps
      `read_only_fast_open == false` so the existing storage
      lifecycle is preserved for mutation.
